title: Framework Compliance Is Non-Negotiable
content: Every piece of work gets a card BEFORE implementation starts. Every card completion is tracked through the SDK (card status, events, quality log). If work is happening without a card, STOP and create one first.

title: Type Contracts Must Be Shared Inline
content: Never let parallel agents define shared types independently. Complete the types card first, include definitions inline in every consumer agent's prompt. Spawning producer + consumer agents simultaneously without shared type contract causes 7+ TypeScript errors post-merge.

title: YAML Round-Trip Is Dangerous for Template Expressions
content: The `yaml` npm library mangles `${{ if }}` / `${{ each }}` expressions, comments, anchors, and quoting. For files with template expressions, use pure string-level insertion instead of parse/serialize.

title: Parameter Naming Must Be Consistent Across Tools
content: In tool sets consumed by LLMs, parameter names must be consistent across tools. LLMs reuse parameter names from one tool's output as another tool's input. Inconsistency causes wrong-value errors.

title: DryRun Is Essential for LLM Workflows
content: LLMs will call destructive tools immediately without user review. Always provide a `dryRun` parameter that returns a full preview without side effects.

title: Absolute Paths Prevent Cross-Tool Confusion
content: Tools that return file paths should use absolute paths. Relative paths cause resolution failures when passed between tools with different working directory contexts.

title: Agent Prompt Design
content: Self-contained prompts with all context inline work reliably (~21-48s). Prompts requiring file reads timeout. Developer agents must NOT use Bash â€” Write/Read tools only. Always include environment constraints block and known antipatterns list.

title: Build Gates After Every Agent
content: Run `tsc --noEmit` (or equivalent) after every agent, not just at card boundaries. Errors compound silently when gates are deferred. Route smoke tests catch runtime errors that type checkers miss.

title: State Persistence Enables Instant Resume
content: SDK-managed state (cards, events, checkpoints) enables instant resume without re-investigation. Context loss without state causes re-orientation and lost nuance. Update card status after every card and write checkpoints before risky operations.

title: Blind Reviewers Truncate - Force-Write Recovery
content: Blind reviewer subagents frequently emit an intermediate thought ('I'll read the files silently before rendering the verdict', 'let me check one more thing') instead of the final verdict. When this happens, use SendMessage to resume the agent with an explicit preamble: 'RESPOND IN ONE COMPLETE MESSAGE with the Blockers/Warnings/Nits format. Do NOT narrate your investigation - go straight to the verdict.' Mention the truncation explicitly in the recovery message. The agent's transcript still has the investigation context so the follow-up cost is small. Observed 3+ times in a single autonomous run against the reviewer subagent type.

title: Schema-Drift Guards on Every External JSON Parse
content: Every JsonElement.TryGetProperty / .GetString chain that touches an upstream API (ARM, Graph, third-party) must handle four shapes explicitly: missing property, null value, wrong ValueKind (e.g. numeric where string expected), and unparseable content (e.g. non-ISO datetime). Choose the bucket each bad shape falls into BEFORE shipping - 'silently skip' leaks data; 'silently include as default' produces misleading reports. The right default is usually 'surface the row under the nearest problematic bucket' so an operator sees there was an input the scanner couldn't classify.
stack: C#, any language parsing external JSON

title: Interface Extraction: Grab the Full Read Surface at Once
content: When extracting an interface from a concrete API client to enable testing, include every read method the client exposes, not just the one method the current slice needs. Single-method interfaces fragment - every subsequent slice adds one more method and the DI binding drifts. Full-surface interfaces are small (5-10 methods for a typical Azure client) and downstream consumers can depend on them without another refactor.

title: CSV Formula Injection - OWASP Single-Quote Prefix
content: Any CSV exported from a web app must neutralise spreadsheet-formula injection on user-sourced string columns. If a cell starts with =, +, -, @, tab, or carriage return, prepend a single-quote (') inside the quoted field so Excel / LibreOffice / Google Sheets read it as literal text rather than evaluating it as a formula. RFC 4180 quoting alone does NOT protect against this - it only protects the column layout. Pattern: if (value[0] matches danger-chars) emit double-quote-single-quote-value-double-quote else emit double-quote-value-double-quote. Applies even to 'safe-looking' columns like framework names that happen to be attacker-controlled today or tomorrow.
stack: Any language exporting CSV from user-influenced data

title: DateTimeOffset.TryParse Needs InvariantCulture + RoundtripKind
content: Calling DateTimeOffset.TryParse(string) without an explicit culture picks up ambient thread culture. A locale-sensitive test runner or a mis-shaped upstream response (e.g. '04/23/2026') may parse to a valid but wrong date, silently routing values into the wrong bucket. When parsing ISO-8601 timestamps from an API, always pass CultureInfo.InvariantCulture and DateTimeStyles.RoundtripKind so only round-trip ISO strings are accepted.

title: Front-load the JSON output contract to stop subagent truncation
content: Long-running subagents (reviewers, developers) truncate mid-analysis and lose their structured deliverable — the reviewer JSON score or the dev decision summary — even though the work is on disk. Cause: open-ended briefs let the agent burn output budget on exploration before emitting the deliverable. Fix: state the JSON/report requirement FIRST in the brief, make it the ONLY mandatory output, cap exploration (max ~5-6 file reads, <=10 min), and instruct 'emit the JSON before any other content if forced to choose'. Reviewers: lead with the JSON block. Developers: COMMIT before writing the summary; summary <=200 words; no file-content dumps. For an already-truncated run, do NOT re-run the card — tight-scope re-spawn an agent that reads the on-disk work and emits ONLY the lost deliverable. Empirically (FRW-BL-025 review, 2026-06-02, Haiku): a JSON-first brief returned a complete parseable verdict with 0 truncations vs >=5 truncations under open-ended briefs the prior night.
